item.title: How to treat NATO allies today
item.description: Edith Pritchett cartoon on President Donald Trump, U.S. allies.

item.title: Rivian and the danger of building a business on government largesse
item.description: The EV market is evolving to survive without public subsidies.

item.title: Remember the metaverse?
item.description: Private companies, no matter how big or successful, are always at the mercy of their customers.

item.title: A narrow-minded attack on James Talarico’s religion
item.description: Progressive Christianity, the Strait of Hormuz and Andy Rooney, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: Iran is a monstrous mistake. This Trump official knew it.
item.description: Israel, the Oscars and living with cancer, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: The danger of letting a mystic lead public health
item.description: A look at Casey Means shows the weakness of her nomination and the surgeon general position itself.

item.title: Bill Barr: Federal judges rely on this guide. It just took a sharp left turn.
item.description: The judiciary needs unbiased scientific evidence, not climate advocacy.

item.title: In Iran, the future of war is coming into view
item.description: Cheap autonomous weapons are overturning the economics of combat — and teaching the U.S. important lessons.

item.title: Schools hiding children’s ‘transitioning’ violate parental rights
item.description: A case involving a Maine student given a chest binder at school may be headed to the Supreme Court.

item.title: A religious asymmetry is unfolding in New York
item.description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani takes his Muslim identity seriously — so should we.

item.title: Medicare-for-all makes a comeback
item.description: But don’t expect any Democrats to have an easier time selling it.

item.title: The real estate developer in chief notches a housing win
item.description: Donald Trump’s executive order captures a market truth. Want more affordable units? Build more.

item.title: China doesn’t want you to know about Operation Yellowbird
item.description: Behind Alysa Liu’s Olympic glory lies a deeper drama — one that China does not want told.

item.title: Would MAGA support financial bailouts? Would Trump?
item.description: If private credit funds collapse, the president’s anti-Wall Street supporters may face a big test.

item.title: The real state of the global financial system might surprise you
item.description: Has the Iran war triggered a financial crisis? Far from it.

item.title: The airports without long security lines
item.description: There’s no reason a dispute over DHS funding should make passenger screening a nightmare.

item.title: An extinction event
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on the $39 trillion U.S. national debt.

item.title: How Trump’s fraud task force succeeds
item.description: The only way to truly root out waste is to reform federal social spending.

item.title: César Chavez and the lies the left tells itself. I know them well.
item.description: Women and girls sexually abused by progressive heroes are discouraged from speaking up.

item.title: How new tech is helping seniors live better, more independent lives
item.description: Innovations are helping people age in place and stay healthier for longer.

item.title: Texas plays favorites in its school choice plan
item.description: Muslim charter schools, TSA workers and the White House’s columns, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: The fate of the NATO alliance lies in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: America’s partners need Persian Gulf oil more than America does. Will they step up?

item.title: The Iran war is metastasizing. Trump needs an endgame.
item.description: Coercion, diplomacy or both — but the president needs to choose a strategy and implement it.

item.title: What happened to Tulsi Gabbard?
item.description: An upcoming Washington Post Opinions show. Stay tuned.

item.title: There’s a reason states run elections
item.description: With the Save America Act, Republicans are making the same mistake Democrats did five years ago.

item.title: How March Madness can make a small town the center of America
item.description: The NCAA tournament belongs to everyone — even the towns you’ve never heard of.

item.title: Europe is distancing itself from America. That leads in one direction.
item.description: Beijing cheers at the prospect of the continent “derisking” itself from Washington.

item.title: After the Iran war, the global economy will never be the same
item.description: The fighting may end, but the inflationary and investment effects across the region will not.
